title: Equilibrium in the AD-AS model
description: Practice what you've learned about modeling a short-run macroeconomic equilibrium in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which point or points in the graph shown here represent a short-run equilibrium level of output?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots three labeled straight lines and six points on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled P L, and the horizontal axis is labeled real G D P. One vertical line is labeled L R A S. One upward-sloping line from the bottom left to the top right is labeled S R A S. One downward-sloping line from top left to bottom right is labeled A D. Point A is located on the L R A S line and intersects a horizontal dashed line from the vertical axis. Point B lies on the L R A S line below point A and intersects another horizontal dashed line. Point D lies at the intersection of the A D and S R A S lines, connected to a horizontal dashed line passing through point A and to a vertical dashed line dropping to point E on the horizontal axis. Point C lies directly below point B on the horizontal axis. Points C and E lie on the horizontal axis below points B and D, respectively.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the price level and real GDP associated with current output in the economy shown in this graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The vertical axis is marked at 102, 105, and 107. The horizontal axis is marked at 190 billion dollars and 200 billion dollars. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis at 200 billion dollars. A D intersects S R A S at a price level of 105 and output of 200 billion. Horizontal dashed lines extend from 102, 105, and 107 to the vertical axis. Vertical dashed lines drop from 190 billion and 200 billion to the horizontal axis.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes a nation that is producing at its short-run macroeconomic equilibrium?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It has a price level and output associated with the intersection of aggregate demand (AD) and short-run aggregate supply (SRAS)
Its unemployment rate is equal to the natural rate of unemployment
Its real GDP and the price level are those at which aggregate demand (AD) equals long-run aggregate supply (LRAS)
It's producing its full employment output
It's producing at a point on its PPC

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes a short-run equilibrium that is not also a long-run equilibrium?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The output and price level associated with the intersection of aggregate demand (AD) and short-run aggregate supply (SRAS)
The output and price level associated with the intersection of aggregate demand (AD) and long-run aggregate supply (LRAS)
The output and price level associated with the intersection of long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) and short-run aggregate supply (SRAS)
The output and price level associated with any point along the aggregate demand (AD) curve
The intersection of aggregate demand (AD), short-run aggregate supply (SRAS), and long run aggregate supply (LRAS)

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph shown here illustrates the current status of the economy in Verminville. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on this graph, which of the following is true about Verminville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled r G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis at Y subscript f, which is aligned with Y subscript 1. A D intersects S R A S at price level P L subscript 1 and output level Y subscript 1. A horizontal dashed line extends from P L subscript 1 to the vertical axis. A vertical dashed line drops from Y subscript 1 to the horizontal axis.

choices.content: The actual unemployment rate equals the natural rate of unemployment
The country has cyclical unemployment but no structural unemployment
The economy is in short-run equilibrium, but not in long-run equilibrium
The current unemployment rate is lower than the natural rate of unemployment
The economy is at a peak in its business cycle

assessment_items.question_content:
Verminville is currently producing an output of $Y_1$ at a price level of $PL_1$, as shown in the graph here.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on this graph, which of the following is true about Verminville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled r G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis at Y subscript f, which is aligned with Y subscript 1. A D intersects S R A S at price level P L subscript 1 and output level Y subscript 1. A horizontal dashed line extends from P L subscript 1 to the vertical axis. A vertical dashed line drops from Y subscript 1 to the horizontal axis.

choices.content: It is in both short-run equilibrium and long-run equilibrium
It is in short-run equilibrium, but not long-run equilibrium
It is in long-run equilibrium, but not short-run equilibrium
It is in neither long-run equilibrium, nor short-run equilibrium
It is in short-run equilibrium, but we cannot determine if it is in long-run equilbrium

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following graphs correctly shows an economy producing its long-run equilibrium level of output?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled r G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis at Y subscript f, which is aligned with Y subscript 1. A D intersects S R A S at price level P L subscript 1 and output level Y subscript 1. A horizontal dashed line extends from P L subscript 1 to the vertical axis. A vertical dashed line drops from Y subscript 1 to the horizontal axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb75c70e2f36326229575de833a0785ff0617c62)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled r G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis at Y subscript f, which is to the left of Y subscript 1. A D intersects S R A S at price level P L subscript 1 and output level Y subscript 1. A horizontal dashed line extends from P L subscript 1 to the vertical axis. A vertical dashed line drops from Y subscript 1 to the horizontal axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/471de24b51cf104c7674fe13f42ef14e16db3a29)

![A coordinate plane plots two labeled straight lines: A D and S R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled r G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. A D intersects S R A S at price level P L subscript 1 and output level Y subscript 1. A horizontal dashed line extends from P L subscript 1 to the vertical axis. A vertical dashed line drops from Y subscript 1 to the horizontal axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/702e7e27cb7fee79128e624b81169aa6e8879c1e)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled r G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis at Y subscript f, which is to the right of Y subscript 1. A D intersects S R A S at price level P L subscript 1 and output level Y subscript 1. A horizontal dashed line extends from P L subscript 1 to the vertical axis. A vertical dashed line drops from Y subscript 1 to the horizontal axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ba315fbd172df442ae20ea8b9e512583e3afcce)

![A coordinate plane plots two labeled straight lines: S R A S and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled r G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis at Y subscript f, which is also labeled Y subscript 1. S R A S intersects L R A S at price level P L subscript 1. A horizontal dashed line extends from P L subscript 1 to the vertical axis. A vertical dashed line drops from Y subscript 1 to the horizontal axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c29164200cf228521f901dcc8714cfe119efd284)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes what happens when a country is in long-run equilibrium?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: AD intersects SRAS and LRAS at the same point.
There is a recessionary gap.
The unemployment rate equals zero percent.
It's at a peak in its business cycle.
There is a positive output gap.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following statements best describes this nation's economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis at Y subscript f, which is to the left of Y subscript 1. A D intersects S R A S at price level P L subscript 1 and output level Y subscript 1. A horizontal dashed line extends from P L subscript 1 to the vertical axis. A vertical dashed line drops from Y subscript 1 to the horizontal axis.

choices.content: The economy is in long-run equilibrium
The economy is experiencing economic growth
The economy has a positive output gap
The economy has a recessionary gap
Real GDP is equal to full employment output

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the graph shown here, which of the following best describes this economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Line L R A S is vertical and positioned to the right of a point labeled Y subscript 1. Line A D intersects S R A S at the vertical line L R A S. A horizontal dashed line extends leftward from the intersection to price level P L subscript 1. A vertical dashed line drops from Y subscript 1 to the horizontal axis.

choices.content: At the origin of their production possibilities curve (PPC)
A point on its production possibilities curve (PPC)
A point beyond its production possibilities curve
A point inside its production possibilities curve
We cannot know where on its production possibilities curve (PPC) that this country is producing

assessment_items.question_content:
The current state of the economy of Dodgeland is represented by the graph shown here:

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following best describes economic conditions in Dodgeland?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are marked with labels 102, 105, and 107 on the vertical axis, and dollar sign 190 b and dollar sign 200 b on the horizontal axis. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects A D and S R A S at dollar sign 200 b and price level 105. Horizontal dashed lines extend leftward from 102, 105, and 107. Vertical dashed lines rise from dollar sign 190 b and dollar sign 200 b to their corresponding intersection points.

choices.content: A positive output gap
A negative output gap
An unemployment rate equal to the natural rate of unemployment
A recession
Long-run equilibrium

assessment_items.question_content:
The value of production at various levels of output are shown in this production possibilities curve (PPC). All values are in millions of dollars.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/da072fcadfe31ad7e2e0d0759eea4222115ada51)

**If current real gross domestic product (GDP) in this country is \$170 million, what is the size of the output gap?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: -\$20 million
\$20 million
-\$60 million
\$10 million
